subject: ENGLISH
title: Detail Tracker
objective: Identify who, where, when, and what in a short original text.
introduction: Important details answer questions such as who, where, when, and what happened. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Detail Tracker”.
A key word used in the lesson “Detail Tracker”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Important details answer questions such as who, where, when, and what happened.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Important details answer questions such as who, where, when, and what happened.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: “At noon, Lani read in the library.” Where was Lani?

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. at noon and reading do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

options.text: Important details answer questions such as who, where, when, and what happened.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Important details answer questions such as who, where, when, and what happened.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Important details answer questions such as who, where, when, and what happened.
The correct response is “Who: Lani” because it follows the lesson explanation: Important details answer questions such as who, where, when, and what happened.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “Adding a person not named” because it follows the lesson explanation: Important details answer questions such as who, where, when, and what happened.

items.prompt: “At noon, Lani read in the library.” Where was Lani?
items.explanation: The correct response is “in the library” because it follows the lesson explanation: Important details answer questions such as who, where, when, and what happened.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “What: returned a book” because it follows the lesson explanation: Important details answer questions such as who, where, when, and what happened.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Detail Tracker; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Detail Tracker.
